question: Big Bash League: Brisbane Heat vs Adelaide Strikers (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Brisbane Heat and Adelaide Strikers scheduled for December 27 2025 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Brisbane Heat vs Adelaide Strikers (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Brisbane Heat Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Brisbane Heat and Adelaide Strikers scheduled for 2025-12-27 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Brisbane Heat will be considered correct if  Brisbane Heat is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Adelaide Strikers will be considered correct if Adelaide Strikers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: SA20: Paarl Royals vs Sunrisers Eastern Cape (Game 1)
description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Joburg Super Kings (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Pretoria Capitals and Joburg Super Kings scheduled for December 27 2025 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Joburg Super Kings (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Pretoria Capitals Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Pretoria Capitals and Joburg Super Kings scheduled for 2025-12-27 in SA20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if  Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if Joburg Super Kings is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: ILT20: Dubai Capitals vs MI Emirates (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Dubai Capitals and MI Emirates scheduled for December 27 2025 in ILT20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: ILT20: Dubai Capitals vs MI Emirates (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Dubai Capitals Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Dubai Capitals and MI Emirates scheduled for 2025-12-27 in International League T20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Dubai Capitals will be considered correct if  Dubai Capitals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to MI Emirates will be considered correct if MI Emirates is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Will the Central Bank of Colombia announce a decrease at the January meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the amount of basis points the target for the monetary policy rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Central Bank of Colombia's January 2026 meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Central Bank of Colombia after its January 30, 2026 policy meeting, as listed on the official Central Bank of Colombia calendar: https://www.banrep.gov.co/es/calendario-eventos

This market may resolve as soon as the Central Bank of Colombia's statement for their January meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

question: Big Bash League: Melbourne Stars vs Sydney Thunder (Game 1) - Most Sixes Melbourne Stars Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Melbourne Stars and Sydney Thunder scheduled for 2025-12-28 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sydney Thunder.The outcome corresponding to Sydney Thunder will be considered correct if Sydney Thunder is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Stars.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Durban's Super Giants vs MI Cape Town (Game 1) - Most Sixes Durban's Super Giants Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Durban's Super Giants and MI Cape Town scheduled for 2025-12-28 in SA20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Durban's Super Giants will be considered correct if Durban's Super Giants is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than MI Cape Town.The outcome corresponding to MI Cape Town will be considered correct if MI Cape Town is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Durban's Super Giants.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.
